question: Will Alex de Minaur be the 2026 Men’s Wimbledon winner?
description:
Wimbledon 2026 is scheduled for June 29 - July 12, 2026.

This market will resolve to the player that wins the 2026 Wimbledon Men’s Singles Tournament.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed player to win the 2026 Wimbledon Men’s Singles Tournament per the rules of the tournament, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 Wimbledon Men’s Singles Tournament is cancelled, postponed after August 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from Wimbledon (https://www.wimbledon.com/index.html); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: 2026 Men’s Wimbledon Winner

eventMetadata.context_description: Carlos Alcaraz and Jannik Sinner's surging dominance on the ATP Tour anchors trader consensus for the 2026 Wimbledon men's winner, with their 38% and 36% implied probabilities reflecting a razor-thin race shaped by youth, grass-court mastery, and consistent elite form. Alcaraz's back-to-back Wimbledon triumphs in 2023-24 highlight his explosive serve-volley game and big-match poise on turf, while Sinner's rise to world No. 1, Australian Open wins, and 2024 semifinal run at SW19 signal rapid grass adaptation despite a hard-court bias. Djokovic's 10% share fades amid age 39 by 2026 and post-knee surgery recovery, underscoring the next-gen shift as recent Masters momentum and clean injury slates keep the duo bunched amid inherent long-term volatility.

Wimbledon 2026 is scheduled for June 29 - July 12, 2026.

This market will resolve to the player that wins the 2026 Wimbledon Women’s Singles Tournament.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed player to win the 2026 Wimbledon Women’s Singles Tournament per the rules of the tournament, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 Wimbledon Women’s Singles Tournament is cancelled, postponed after August 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from Wimbledon (https://www.wimbledon.com/index.html); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: 2026 Women's Wimbledon Winner
events.description:
Wimbledon 2026 is scheduled for June 29 - July 12, 2026.

This market will resolve to the player that wins the 2026 Wimbledon Women’s Singles Tournament.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed player to win the 2026 Wimbledon Women’s Singles Tournament per the rules of the tournament, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 Wimbledon Women’s Singles Tournament is cancelled, postponed after August 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from Wimbledon (https://www.wimbledon.com/index.html); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus pins Aryna Sabalenka as the slim favorite at 27% implied probability for the 2026 Women's Wimbledon crown, buoyed by her blistering baseline power and grass-adapted aggression, yet Elena Rybakina's 19.5% and Iga Świątek's 19.3% keep odds bunched amid the surface's serve-dominated volatility and upset-prone history. Rybakina's 2022 title and towering delivery counter Sabalenka's injury vulnerabilities—highlighted by her 2024 Wimbledon withdrawal—while Świątek's clay-to-grass evolution and major dominance add ballast. Over two years, roster flux, youth surges like Mirra Andreeva, and rest advantages will reshape dynamics, mirroring Wimbledon's tradition of parity where no player owns the lawns.

eventMetadata.context_description: Carlos Alcaraz tops trader consensus at 37.5% implied probability for the 2026 US Open men's title, driven by his all-surface dominance, including four Grand Slam wins by age 21 and strong hard-court showings like the 2022 US Open victory. Jannik Sinner trails closely at 28.5%, bolstered by his recent 2024 US Open triumph, ascent to world No. 1, and clearance from doping scrutiny, signaling sustained elite form on fast courts. Novak Djokovic lingers at 7.3% amid his record 24 majors but tempered by age 39 and a shaky 2024 hard-court campaign post-knee surgery. Lower tiers like Zverev and Medvedev reflect consistent top-10 presence without breakthrough Slam momentum, while emerging Americans Shelton and Draper add home-crowd intrigue but lack proven major pedigree. Recent Shanghai Masters results and Davis Cup qualifiers underscore the Big Two's edge in depth and recovery from minor ailments.

eventMetadata.context_description: Aryna Sabalenka tops trader consensus at 35.5% for the 2026 US Open women's crown, propelled by her hard-court dominance—highlighted by Australian Open titles and a power baseline game suiting New York's speedy surface—while Elena Rybakina's 20.5% reflects her booming serve and consistent deep runs in North American swing events like Indian Wells. Iga Swiatek trails at 11.5% despite world No. 1 status, as her clay bias and recent grass quarterfinal exits at Wimbledon underscore hard-court question marks. Coco Gauff's 5.1% nods to her 2023 title defense potential and home-crowd boost, but youth risers like Mirra Andreeva (3.6%) draw bets on breakout form post-French Open semis; post-Wimbledon shifts favor hard specialists amid Olympic clay distractions.

question: 100kt meteor strike in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a natural meteoroid (bolide) explodes in Earth's atmosphere with a total impact energy greater than or equal to 100 kilotons of TNT equivalent between January 1 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The object must be classified as a natural meteoroid; events involving artificial objects or reentry vehicles do not qualify.

The primary resolution source will be the NASA JPL Fireball and Bolide Data repository: https://cneos.jpl.nasa.gov/fireballs/. The relevant field for determining impact energy is the “Impact Energy (kt)” column. If this dataset has not been updated to include all relevant dates by February 28, 2027, or if the NASA JPL Fireball and Bolide Data repository becomes permanently unavailable, this market may resolve based on a consensus of credible sources including the European Space Agency (ESA), the International Asteroid Warning Network (IAWN), the U.S. Department of Defense, or credible reporting of a scientific consensus, such as a NASA press release.

events.title: 100kt meteor strike in 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a natural meteoroid (bolide) explodes in Earth's atmosphere with a total impact energy greater than or equal to 100 kilotons of TNT equivalent between January 1 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The object must be classified as a natural meteoroid; events involving artificial objects or reentry vehicles do not qualify.

The primary resolution source will be the NASA JPL Fireball and Bolide Data repository: https://cneos.jpl.nasa.gov/fireballs/. The relevant field for determining impact energy is the “Impact Energy (kt)” column. If this dataset has not been updated to include all relevant dates by February 28, 2027, or if the NASA JPL Fireball and Bolide Data repository becomes permanently unavailable, this market may resolve based on a consensus of credible sources including the European Space Agency (ESA), the International Asteroid Warning Network (IAWN), the U.S. Department of Defense, or credible reporting of a scientific consensus, such as a NASA press release.

eventMetadata.context_description: NASA's Center for Near-Earth Object Studies (CNEOS) tracks all known asteroids and comets, with its Sentry system showing zero potential impacts in 2026 exceeding 100 kilotons TNT-equivalent energy—the explosive yield of a roughly 15-20 meter object like the 2013 Chelyabinsk airburst. Comprehensive surveys by Pan-STARRS, ATLAS, and NEOWISE have cataloged over 99% of kilometer-scale threats and most hazardous smaller near-Earth objects (NEOs), confirming no collision trajectories for next year. Historical bolide data from government sensors indicate such events occur about once per decade worldwide, yielding a baseline annual probability below 0.1%. Trader sentiment at 95.7% "No" reflects this robust monitoring, though a realistic low-probability scenario involves an undetected small, fast-moving asteroid spotted late via ground-based telescopes.

description:

eventMetadata.context_description: NASA's Center for Near-Earth Object Studies (CNEOS) and Sentry impact monitoring system show no known asteroids or meteoroids on a collision course with Earth in 2026 capable of delivering 1 megaton of explosive energy, equivalent to about twice the Chelyabinsk meteor's 2013 airburst. With over 95% of near-Earth objects larger than 140 meters cataloged and smaller bolides routinely tracked via telescopes and fireball networks, the baseline annual probability of such an event is roughly 1 in several million, driving the market's 97.8% "No" consensus. Realistic risks remain negligible but include an undetected ~25-meter object evading surveys, though recent ATLAS and Pan-STARRS discoveries underscore improving detection amid zero 2026 threats on NASA's risk table.

question: Will Tesla deliver less than 350000 vehicles in Q1 2026
description:
This market will resolve according to Tesla's announced total vehicle deliveries for Q1 2026.

If Tesla does not publish Q1 2026 delivery figures by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest bracket.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The resolution source will be https://ir.tesla.com/press.

events.title: How many Tesla deliveries in Q1 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to Tesla's announced total vehicle deliveries for Q1 2026.

If Tesla does not publish Q1 2026 delivery figures by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest bracket.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors under 350,000 Tesla vehicle deliveries in Q1 2026 at 65% implied probability, driven by persistent EV demand weakness and production scaling challenges amid intensifying competition from BYD and legacy automakers. Recent Q1 2025 deliveries of just 336,789—down 13% year-over-year—highlighted seasonal softness exacerbated by high interest rates and inventory buildup, with Cybertruck volumes ramping slower than anticipated despite Texas Gigafactory expansions. Elon Musk's guidance for 20-30% full-year 2025 growth implies quarterly averages around 500,000, but Q1 seasonality and delays in the affordable next-gen model tilt odds toward the lower bins, with traders eyeing Q4 2025 earnings for updated 2026 forecasts.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors under 350,000 Tesla vehicle deliveries in Q1 2026 at 65% implied probability, driven by persistent EV demand weakness and production scaling challenges amid intensifying competition from BYD and legacy automakers. Recent Q1 2025 deliveries of just 336,789—down 13% year-over-year—highlighted seasonal softness exacerbated by high interest rates and inventory buildup, with Cybertruck volumes ramping slower than anticipated despite Texas Gigafactory expansions. Elon Musk's guidance for 20-30% full-year 2025 growth implies quarterly averages around 500,000, but Q1 seasonality and delays in the affordable next-gen model tilt odds toward the lower bins, with traders eyeing Q4 2025 earnings for updated 2026 forecasts.

question: Will Alcaraz or Sinner win more Grand Slams in 2026?
description:
This is a market to predict whether Carlos Alcaraz or Jannik Sinner wins more Grand Slam championships in 2026.

If Carlos Alcaraz wins more Grand Slams in 2026 than Jannik Sinner, this market will resolve to “Alcaraz”.

If Jannik Sinner wins more Grand Slams in 2026 than Carlos Alcaraz, this market will resolve to “Sinner”.

If Alcaraz and Sinner win the same amount of Grand Slams in 2026, this market will resolve 50-50.

Only victories in Men’s Singles Grand Slam tournaments will be considered for this market’s resolution. The relevant 2026 Grand Slams are the Men’s Singles tournaments in the 2026 Australian Open, French Open, U.S. Open, and Wimbledon.

If it becomes impossible for either Alcaraz or Sinner to win as many Grand Slams in 2026 as the other (e.g. Alcaraz wins the first three grand slams), this market will resolve immediately. Otherwise, this market will resolve when the results of the final 2026 Grand Slam are official.

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from the organizers of the Grand Slam tournaments; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Carlos Alcaraz at 79% implied probability to outpace Jannik Sinner in 2026 Grand Slam titles, driven primarily by Alcaraz's unmatched versatility across surfaces—boasting wins at Roland Garros (clay), Wimbledon (grass), and US Open (hard)—contrasting Sinner's dominance limited mostly to hardcourts with Australian Open and US Open 2024 triumphs. Recent form underscores this: Sinner's stellar year-end surge and world No. 1 ranking reflect hardcourt prowess, yet Alcaraz's Paris Olympics gold and head-to-head edge (6-4 lifetime) highlight big-match resilience. Both young stars (21 and 23) appear injury-free post-recoveries, but Alcaraz's all-court adaptability better positions him for multi-Slam potential amid unpredictable draws and fatigue factors.

question: Will OpenAI acquire Pinterest in 2026?
description:
is market will resolve to "Yes" if it is officially announced that Pinterest will be, has been, or is being acquired by or merged with OpenAI by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An announcement by Pinterest will qualify for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether the announced acquisition/merger actually occurs.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Pinterest or OpenAi, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors "No" at 93% implied probability for OpenAI acquiring Pinterest in 2026, driven primarily by the absence of any credible rumors, official statements, or strategic leaks indicating interest from either party. OpenAI remains laser-focused on advancing frontier AI models like GPT series and infrastructure scaling, with no history of consumer social media acquisitions amid its Microsoft-backed structure and regulatory scrutiny on mega-deals. Pinterest, valued at around $25 billion market cap, operates in visual discovery without evident synergies to OpenAI's core generative tech despite potential DALL-E image overlaps. Realistic challenges include a surprise partnership escalation or OpenAI pivoting to consumer apps post-fundraise, though such shifts contradict current trajectories and face antitrust hurdles.

question: Ethereum flipped in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Ethereum (ETH) is not the second or first largest cryptocurrency by market capitalization at any point between January 1, and December 31, 2026 ET, according to CoinGecko. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source will be CoinGecko. If CoinGecko becomes permanently unavailable, another credible source will be chosen. If there is ambiguity, a consensus of credible reporting will be used.

question: Will Joshua Van be the UFC Flyweight Champion on December 31, 2026?
description:

Only official UFC division champions will count. Interim champions will not count.

eventMetadata.context_description: Alexandre Pantoja's unanimous decision victory over Brandon Royval at UFC 310 on December 7 solidifies his grip as defending flyweight champion, anchoring trader consensus at 40%, yet the market remains tightly bunched due to surging contenders. eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus pins Alexander Volkanovski as the frontrunner to hold UFC featherweight gold by end-2026, leveraging his No. 1 ranking, two prior title reigns, and resilience after consecutive losses to champion Ilia Topuria, who hinted at a lightweight move following his first-round knockout of Max Holloway at UFC 308. Lerone Murphy and Movsar Evloev share strong support at 27.5% each, driven by their unblemished records—Murphy's 15-0-1 with a unanimous decision over Edson Barboza, Evloev's 19-0—and their December 7 headliner positioning the winner as top contender. Jean Silva's 19.5% reflects his explosive five-fight knockout streak, marking him as a rising threat amid the division's youth movement.

Jean Silva's 19.5% reflects his explosive five-fight knockout streak, marking him as a rising threat amid the division's youth movement.

question: New Coronavirus Pandemic in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the World Health Organization (WHO) declares a new COVID pandemic other than COVID-19 (SARS-CoV-2) between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source will be official announcements from the World Health Organization.

events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the World Health Organization (WHO) declares a new COVID pandemic other than COVID-19 (SARS-CoV-2) between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader conviction favors "No" at 92.5% for a new coronavirus pandemic in 2026, driven by widespread population immunity from prior infections and vaccines—over 70% global coverage per WHO data—coupled with advanced genomic surveillance detecting variants early. CDC wastewater monitoring and hospital metrics confirm COVID-19's endemic status, with current strains like KP.3 causing mild, seasonal waves rather than exponential surges. Rapid mRNA vaccine updates further bolster defenses, mirroring successful containment of past threats. Realistic challenges include a novel zoonotic spillover evading existing antibodies or undetected immune-escape mutations, though historical precedents and global networks make such events improbable within the year.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus crowns Ilia Topuria as the overwhelming favorite to hold UFC lightweight gold by end of 2026, propelled by his devastating third-round knockout of Max Holloway at UFC 308, affirming his elite striking power and youth at 27. Topuria's vocal pursuit of Islam Makhachev underscores a likely division shakeup, with implied doubts on the champ's prolonged dominance amid welterweight ambitions. Arman Tsarukyan's 20% stake stems from his gritty decision win over Charles Oliveira at UFC 300 and near-upset of Makhachev, marking him as prime contender material. Veterans Justin Gaethje and Oliveira linger in double digits on knockout pedigree and submission threat, though recent losses temper expectations amid rising stars like Benoît Saint Denis.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Islam Makhachev at 63% to claim UFC welterweight gold by end-2026, driven by his unparalleled 15-fight win streak as lightweight champion and speculation he'll move up after dominating Dustin Poirier at UFC 302, leveraging Dagestani wrestling against aging or unproven 170-pounders. Carlos Prates' 24.5% reflects his blistering four-fight UFC knockout streak, including finishes over Neil Magny and Li Jingliang, positioning him as a dark horse striker. Ian Machado Garry's 18% stems from his perfect 15-0 record and stylistic win over Geoff Neal, while Michael Morales (8%) gains from prospect hype post-17-0 run. Belal Muhammad's title reign after dethroning Leon Edwards at UFC 304 holds low traction at 1%, amid doubts on his finishing power and upcoming tests like Shavkat Rakhmonov.

events.description:
This market will resolve according to the official UFC Welterweight division champion on December 31, 2026 at 12:00 PM ET.

Only official UFC division champions will count. Interim champions will not count.

If the relevant belt is vacant at this market’s check time, or there is otherwise no champion in the specified division, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC (https://www.ufc.com/athletes).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Islam Makhachev at 63% to claim UFC welterweight gold by end-2026, driven by his unparalleled 15-fight win streak as lightweight champion and speculation he'll move up after dominating Dustin Poirier at UFC 302, leveraging Dagestani wrestling against aging or unproven 170-pounders. Carlos Prates' 24.5% reflects his blistering four-fight UFC knockout streak, including finishes over Neil Magny and Li Jingliang, positioning him as a dark horse striker. Ian Machado Garry's 18% stems from his perfect 15-0 record and stylistic win over Geoff Neal, while Michael Morales (8%) gains from prospect hype post-17-0 run. Belal Muhammad's title reign after dethroning Leon Edwards at UFC 304 holds low traction at 1%, amid doubts on his finishing power and upcoming tests like Shavkat Rakhmonov.
